items.title: Pope made us feel that writers are useful says Igiaba Scego
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - Pope Leo XIV made an international delegation of authors feel that writers are useful during an audience Wednesday marking the 100th anniversary of the founding of Vatican publisher Libreria Editrice Vataicnaa (LEV), Italo-Somali novelist Igiaba Scego told ANSA after their encounter with the American pontiff. "Pope Leo made us feel that writers are very useful. Fiction is often marginalized, and yet, precisely because we live in a world of artificial intelligence and unbridled capitalism, understanding that storytelling is the center of life was important. The Pope's wo
items.titleIt: Pope made us feel that writers are useful says Igiaba Scego

items.title: Regeni family faced 'persecution, threats', trial of Egyptian officials told
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - Alessandra Ballerini, the lawyer representing Giulio Regeni's parents, told the Rome in-absentia trial of four Egyptian security officials accused of torturing the Italian student to death in 2016 that the family had faced threats and intimidation during their battle for justice. "The Regeni family fought with their bare hands against the Egyptian regime, but never lost a shred of dignity," Ballerini told a hearing in the bunker courtroom of Rome's Rebibbia prison. "The Regeni family never allowed themselves to be consumed by hatred. "They have never sought revenge or p
items.titleIt: Regeni family faced 'persecution, threats', trial of Egyptian officials told

items.title: PM's office asks 2 mn euro Regeni compensation
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - Premier Giorgia Meloni's office on Wednesday requested compensation of two million euros from four Egyptian national security officials charged with abducting and torturing to death Italian student Giulio Regeni in Cairo in January-February 2016. Speaking at their in-absentia trial in Rome, the State Attorney said that "this crime has profoundly affected the Italian community" and that the defendants "have violated the rights to the protection of Italian citizens' freedoms, even abroad. "Constitutionally guaranteed rights have been violated." The 28-year-old student's h
items.titleIt: PM's office asks 2 mn euro Regeni compensation

items.title: Prosecutors ask a life term and three 17-and-a-half-year terms for Regeni murder
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 23 - Rome prosecutors on Tuesday requested a life term and three 17-and-a-half-year terms for four Egyptian security officials in their in-absentia trial in Rome for torturing to death Italian student Guiulio Regeni in January-February 2016. The 28-year-old student's half-naked body was found in a ditch on the Cairo-Alexandria highway on February 3, 2016, a week after he disappeared on the Cairo metro on January 25. Regeni, a Friuli-born Cambridge University doctoral researcher into Cairo street unions, was allegedly abducted and tortured to death by National Security Genera
items.titleIt: Prosecutors ask a life term and three 17-and-a-half-year terms for Regeni murder

items.title: Egypt chose to protect Regeni's killers says Rome prosecutor
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 23 - Egypt chose to protect the men who tortured Giulio Regeni to death rather than make them face justice, Rome Deputy Chief Prosecutor Sergio Colaiocco said on Tuesday in his closing arguments in the in-absentia trial of four members of the Egyptian security services for the homicide. The 28-year-old student's half-naked body was found in a ditch on the Cairo-Alexandria highway on February 3, 2016, a week after he disappeared on the Cairo metro on January 25. "A body shattered by pain," Colaiocco said as he showed images of a CAT scan of Regeni's corpse. "This is precisely
items.titleIt: Egypt chose to protect Regeni's killers says Rome prosecutor

items.title: Italian Cooperation Supports Surgical Services at Cairo Hospital
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 23 GIU - Italian Cooperation, together with the University of Molise and the Egyptian Ministry of Health, is supporting the improvement of surgical services at the Umberto I Hospital in Cairo. The Italian hospital is considered a model of excellence in healthcare for all of Egypt. The goal is to modernize the operating rooms and strengthen the skills of healthcare professionals. Read article...
items.titleIt: Italian Cooperation Supports Surgical Services at Cairo Hospital

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Ex Berlusconi villa in Sardinia sold to Qatari sheikh, ex PM
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - The former Sardinian luxury villa of late premier Silvio Berlusconi has been sold to Qatari Sheikh Hamad bin Jassim bin Jaber Al Thani, the former prime minister of the Gulf emirate, the Berlusconi family holding company Fininvest said Wednesday, confirming media reports. Villa Certosa has been sold to the royal family's Constellation real estate group for an undisclosed sum, it said. The Sultan of Brunei had also voiced interest in the 500 million euro villa, for decades one of the most famous private estates in Europe. Located on the stunning coastline of Sardinia, th
items.titleIt: Ex Berlusconi villa in Sardinia sold to Qatari sheikh, ex PM

items.title: Libya: Eastern Libya Bans Entry of Nationals From Four African Countries Amid Migration Crackdown
items.description: [Shabelle] Benghazi, Libya, June 24, 2026 -- Authorities in eastern Libya have banned the entry of nationals from Sudan, Eritrea, Ethiopia and Somalia, saying the measure is part of efforts to reorganize and regulate the flow of foreign nationals entering the country.
items.titleIt: Libya: Eastern Libya Bans Entry of Nationals From Four African Countries Amid Migration Crackdown

items.title: Sudan: Sudan War - Int'l Pressure Grows As Fears Mount Over Escalation in El Obeid
items.description: [Dabanga] Kampala / El Obeid / Amsterdam -- International efforts to address Sudan's deepening crisis have intensified amid growing warnings from the United Nations and foreign governments about an expanding conflict and worsening humanitarian conditions, particularly in the city of El Obeid, capital of North Kordofan. As previously reported by Radio Dabanga, El Obeid has become the focus of mounting international concern, with diplomats and aid agencies warning that it could face a similar fate to El Fasher, where months of fighting have been
items.titleIt: Sudan: Sudan War - Int'l Pressure Grows As Fears Mount Over Escalation in El Obeid

items.title: Sudan: Opposition Grows to Proposed Chinese Copper Mining in Sudan's Red Sea State
items.description: [Dabanga] Port Sudan -- Opposition is mounting in eastern Sudan over reports that the government is preparing to sign a long-term copper mining agreement with a Chinese company, with local political groups warning that the country's natural resources should not be committed while war continues and key questions about Sudan's future remain unresolved.
items.titleIt: Sudan: Opposition Grows to Proposed Chinese Copper Mining in Sudan's Red Sea State

items.title: Rutte's statements on US flights from Italy during Iran war misleading - defence min
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - The defence ministry said Wednesday that NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte's assertion that 500 US flights took off from American bases in Italy during Operation Epic Fury was misleading, stressing that Rome had only authorized the bases to be used for ordinary operations during the Iran war and not for offensive combat missions. Rutte's comments sparked a political row here, with opposition politicians saying they conflict with the government's statements about the use of the bases and calling on Premier Giorgia Meloni to report to parliament to clarify. During the war
items.titleIt: Rutte's statements on US flights from Italy during Iran war misleading - defence min

items.title: Ambassador Fontana Meets with Iraqi Minister of Industry
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 24 GIU - As part of the ongoing economic dialogue between Italy and Iraq, the Italian Ambassador to Baghdad, Niccolò Fontana, met with the Iraqi Minister of Industry and Mineral Resources, Mohammed Nouri Ahmed. The meeting provided an opportunity to explore prospects for strengthening bilateral industrial cooperation and to examine opportunities for collaboration and investment in strategic sectors such as the chemical industry, silicates, cement, silica sand, and the pharmaceutical sector. The meeting reaffirmed the shared commitment to further developing economic and industria
items.titleIt: Ambassador Fontana Meets with Iraqi Minister of Industry
